info.title: Valid Part API
info.description:
Searches by **part number** to return **detailed part information**, including **alternate units of measure** for bulk items.

The legacy SOAP **Is Valid Part** operation maps to this REST API (**Valid Part API**). Use your **SOAP-to-REST field mapping** and **REST API FAQ** attachments for property-level details.

Support: Warranty_Api_Support@JohnDeere.com

servers.description: Sandbox (cert). Production uses `https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com` — confirm the path prefix under `/digcust/wy/` and whether the `cert` segment is present for your environment.

OAuth2.description: John Deere Dealer Solutions OAuth 2.0. Use the **authorization code** flow (three-legged) for interactive users, or the **client credentials** grant for machine-to-machine access. Request the OAuth scope assigned to your application for this API (this reference uses `warranty` as an example — confirm the scope in your Developer.Deere.com application and technical documentation).

PartEntity.description: OData **Part** entity from **PartsSet** / related **PartData**. Exact properties follow your OData contract and SOAP-to-REST mapping; additional fields may appear.

partNumber.description: Part number (or material number) when returned.

isValid.description: Whether the part is valid for warranty or catalog use when exposed by the service.

alternateUnitsOfMeasure.description: Alternate units of measure for bulk or packaged items when returned (shape per your mapping).

PartsSetCollection.description: OData collection returned from **PartsSet** (may include `@odata.context` and other OData annotations).

value.description: Part entities matching the request or query.

ODataBatchResponse.description: OData **$batch** response as **multipart/mixed** MIME parts (per OData batch protocol).

message.example: No part was found for the supplied part number.

get.summary: Get part is valid or not (single part by key)
get.description:
Retrieves **Part** details from **PartsSet** using the OData-style key **PartNum** — the REST mapping for **Get Part Is Valid Or Not**.

**Sandbox example:** `GET https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/WarrantyDBSVALIDPART/PartsSet('RE123456')`

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program.

**Accept:** `application/json`

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `WarrantyDBSVALIDPART`).

parameters.name: PartNum
parameters.description: Part number in the PartsSet key (OData single-quoted key segment).

200.description: Part details returned for the supplied part number.

400.description: Bad request — malformed key or invalid parameters.

error.message: The PartsSet key could not be parsed.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.

404.description: No part found for the supplied part number.

error.message: No part was found for the supplied part number.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

get.description:
Reads the **PartsSet** entity set — the REST mapping for **Get entities from related PartData**. You may combine this with OData query options (`$filter`, `$top`, `$skip`, etc.) when supported by the service.

**Sandbox example:** `GET https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/WarrantyDBSVALIDPART/PartsSet`

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program.

**Accept:** `application/json`

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `WarrantyDBSVALIDPART`).

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description: OData **$filter** expression when supported by the service.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Maximum number of entities to return.

400.description: Bad request — invalid OData query options.

error.message: The query could not be processed.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

post.description:
Groups multiple OData requests into a **single** HTTP request using the **$batch** endpoint. See [OData batch requests](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_BatchRequests). Request and response bodies use **multipart/mixed** per OData batch semantics.

**Sandbox example:** `POST https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/WarrantyDBSVALIDPART/$batch`

**Accept / Content-Type:** `multipart/mixed` (include a valid MIME **boundary** in the Content-Type header for the request body).

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program.

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `WarrantyDBSVALIDPART`).

requestBody.description: OData batch request body as **multipart/mixed** (batch boundary and MIME parts per OData specification).

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST \
  --url "https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/WarrantyDBSVALIDPART/$batch" \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' \
  --header 'Accept: multipart/mixed' \
  --header 'Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=batch_12345' \
  --data-binary @batch-request.mime

200.description: Batch completed; response is **multipart/mixed** containing per-request results.

202.description: Batch accepted (when the service returns 202 for async batch processing).

400.description: Bad request — malformed batch payload or boundary.

error.message: The batch request could not be parsed.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.
